response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack software developer with strong web, mobile, and backend experience, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no experience with chip design, performance modeling for deep-learning workloads, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or any AI accelerator work. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments was on a desktop IDE, not hardware modeling. Educational background (BS CS) would require 8+ years in a relevant field, which is not demonstrated here.
strengths: Long track record of shipping production software across diverse stacks
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang microservices and C/C++ at National Instruments
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, public talks)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware design, or AI accelerator experience
No deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Career is in web/full-stack application development, not hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware experience
No AI accelerator or DNN performance optimization background
Strong general software engineering track record
